basics.name: Xian M. D. Hadia
basics.label: PhD Candidate in Computational Science and Engineering
basics.summary: Engineer-in-Training (EIT) and PhD Candidate advised by Raphaël Pestourie at Georgia Tech, conducting research on inverse design in nanophotonics. Skilled in electromagnetic simulation, adjoint optimization, and surrogate modeling with hands-on laboratory experience.

location.address: School of Computational Science and Engineering

work.name: Georgia Institute of Technology
work.summary: Conducting research on inverse design in nanophotonics and teaching graduate courses.
highlights: Designed metalenses for focusing, holography, and wavelength multiplexing using adjoint-based optimization
Developed high-fidelity physics simulations (FDTD, RCWA) using COMSOL and custom Julia/Python code
Trained and deployed surrogate models for electromagnetic scattering and heat transfer problems
Taught computational photography and machine learning to graduate students

work.name: Corning Inc.
work.summary: Automated optical metrology systems and developed software interfaces for precision measurement.
highlights: Automated interferometer system integrating laser, optical switches, and data acquisition
Developed software interfaces for device control and synchronized communication protocols
Built user-friendly applications for technicians to configure tests and automate strain calculations
Reduced test time by 8× and transformed multi-operator workflow to single-operator process

work.name: U.S. Army Corps of Engineers
work.summary: Designed and deployed advanced measurement systems in field environments and anechoic chambers.
highlights: Designed and deployed measurement systems (antennas, cameras, signal generators, frequency analyzers)
Designed and implemented ground-based synthetic aperture radar (SAR) rail system
Developed high-fidelity lidar and radar sensor models for autonomous-vehicle simulations

work.name: Mississippi College
work.summary: Assisted in teaching physics, circuit theory, and microcontrollers to undergraduate students.
highlights: Taught physics, circuit theory, and microcontrollers
Led hands-on lab sessions and mentored students in circuit troubleshooting

publications.name: Harnessing Data Efficiency with a Fixed-Point Iteration Analogous to Adaptive Mesh Refinement
publications.publisher: SIAM Conference on Mathematics of Data Science (MDS24)
publications.summary: Poster presented at SIAM MDS24, Atlanta, GA

publications.name: Improving Surrogate Model Performance by Training Neural Operators on Full PDE Solution Data
publications.summary: Co-authored with R. Pestourie and L. Lu
